title: CAPIT Methodology | Sources, Verification, and Publication Rules
description: Learn how CAPIT collects, normalizes, verifies, and publishes well-plugging records and related protocol activity.
headline: CAPIT Methodology
body: This page explains how CAPIT collects source records, applies publication rules, handles revisions, and links public data to protocol activity.

sections.heading: Source Priorities
sections.body: CAPIT prioritizes official source agencies, approved public datasets, and documented publication logic. Where definitions differ across jurisdictions, CAPIT preserves the source definition and makes normalization visible.

sections.heading: Publication Rules
sections.body: A record is published only after CAPIT’s source, formatting, and verification requirements are satisfied. Publication rules should be versioned and publicly dated.

sections.heading: Revisions
sections.body: Public records can change. CAPIT preserves a visible revision history where source agencies correct, reclassify, or expand underlying records.

sections.heading: Minting Logic
sections.body: CAPIT should publish the approved minting rule, version date, and relevant contract references here. If the rule changes, the page should preserve prior versions and effective dates.

sections.heading: Important Boundaries
sections.body: CAPIT’s methodology explains how records are published and how protocol activity is linked. It does not convert the token into a carbon credit, security, ownership claim, or direct remediation funding instrument unless separate legal documentation expressly says so.
